basic_details.job_title: ITBP Assistant Commandant Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 06 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Indo-Tibetan Border Police Force (ITBP), Ministry of Home Affairs
basic_details.short_information: The latest Indo-Tibetan Border Police Force (ITBP) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Assistant Commandant (Engineer) posts! Published on the official website recruitment.itbpolice.nic.in, this new recruitment drive offers 6 vacancies for eligible male and female candidates. Applicants within the age limit of up to 30 years and possessing a Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering from a recognized university or institute can apply online from 21 April 2026 to 20 May 2026. Find all details about the ITBP Assistant Commandant (Engineer) new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online form, a

application_fee.details: The application fee must be paid online only through the payment gateway available at the official ITBP recruitment website. Payment through any other mode will not be accepted and applications submitted with alternate payment modes will be summarily rejected. The fee once paid is non-refundable under all circumstances, including in the event of cancellation of notified vacancies. | SC/ST candidates, Ex-Servicemen, and female candidates are fully exempted from the application fee. Candidates are advised to complete the payment process carefully and retain the payment confirmation for their records.

qualification.details: Educational Qualification: | Age Limit & Relaxation: | Physical Standards: | Medical Standards:

salary_details.pay scale: Level-10 in Pay Matrix: ₹56,100 – ₹1,77,500 (as per 7th CPC)
salary_details.special compensatory allowance: Applicable while posted in specified border areas
salary_details.medical facilities: Free medical facilities for self and family
salary_details.details: The Assistant Commandant (Engineer) post carries a very attractive pay package under the 7th Central Pay Commission. The basic pay is at Level-10 in the Pay Matrix ranging from ₹56,100 to ₹1,77,500. In addition to the basic pay, the post carries Dearness Allowance (DA), Special Compensatory Allowance (applicable while posted in specified border areas), House Rent Allowance (HRA), Transport Allowance, Leave Travel Concession (LTC), and Free Medical Facilities for the officer and family. As per the posting location (border/non-border), additional allowances are admissible as per Force rules. | On appointment, candidates will have the option to choose either the New Pension Scheme (NPS) introduced by the Government of India with effect from 01.01.2004, or the Unified Pension Scheme (UPS) introduced with effect from 01.04.2024, as per their convenience. Candidates who resign before completing a minimum service of 10 years will be required to refund an amount equal to three months' pay and allowances or the cost of training, whichever is higher.

selection_process: 1. Physical Standard Test (PST) Height, Chest, Weight as per prescribed norms
2. Physical Efficiency Test (PET) Qualifying in nature - all events must be independently cleared
3. Biometric Identification Mandatory at PST/PET; verified at all subsequent stages
4. Written Examination Paper-I (MCQ/CBT) - 100 marks, 2 hours; Paper-II (Subjective) - 100 marks, 2 hours
5. Document Verification Original documents checked by Board of Officers before interview
6. Interview (Personality Test) 50 marks - Knowledge, Experience, Personality, GK, Extra-curricular
7. Merit List Based on combined marks of Written Exam + Interview
8. Detailed Medical Examination (DME) Conducted as per MHA/ADG (Med) CAPFs guidelines
9. Review Medical Examination (RME) Available for candidates declared unfit in DME, on written consent within 24 hours
800 Meter Race 3 min 45 seconds 4 min 45 seconds
Long Jump 3.5 meters (3 chances) 3.0 meters (3 chances)
Shot Put (7.26 Kg) 4.5 meters (3 chances) N/A
Civil Engineering 15 questions Part-A: 5 questions × 2 marks; Part-B: 5 questions × 8 marks; Part-C: 5 questions × 10 marks
Profession-related Experience 10 (scaled by duration of experience)
The selection process for ITBP Assistant Commandant (Engineer) is comprehensive and multi-staged
and overall personality. All stages are qualifying/evaluative
and candidates must clear each stage to proceed to the next. The process begins with a Physical Standard Test (PST) and Physical Efficiency Test (PET)
followed by a Written Examination (two papers)
and finally a Review Medical Examination (RME) for those declared unfit in DME.

The final merit list is prepared based on combined marks of the Written Examination and Interview. Biometric enrolment is mandatory at the PST/PET stage and may be verified at any subsequent stage. Candidates must carry a clear printed copy of their online application form and admit card to enter the PST/PET venue.
Minimum Qualifying Marks: 50% for UR/OBC/Ex-Servicemen/EWS; 45% for SC/ST in each paper.
how_to_apply: The application process is entirely online through the official ITBP recruitment website. Candidates must fill the online application form carefully after reading all instructions. A genuine and functional email ID and active mobile number must be provided during registration, as all communications including admit cards will be sent through these channels.

All information filled in the form - including gender, category (UR/SC/ST/OBC(NCL)/EWS), and employment status - must be accurate and complete, as any false or incorrect information will lead to cancellation of candidature with no option for change at later stages. In-service candidates working in Central/State Government, Autonomous Bodies, PSUs, or Statutory Bodies must submit a No Objection Certificate (NOC) in original as per Annexure-V from their employer at the time of document verification; failure to produce NOC will result in summary rejection of candidature. Candidates are strongly advised to apply well before the closing date to avoid any last-minute technical issues.
Online application window: 21/04/2026 (00:01 AM) to 20/05/2026 (11:59 PM)
Fill all mandatory fields including gender, category, employment status accurately
Pay application fee online (UR/OBC/EWS: ₹400; SC/ST/Ex-Servicemen/Female: Nil)
Provide valid email ID and mobile number - all communications sent only through these
Take a clear printout of the completed online application form and bring it to PST/PET
In-service candidates must produce original NOC at the time of documentation
important_instructions: Candidates must read the complete notification carefully before applying and ensure they satisfy all eligibility conditions. The recruitment in ITBP is entirely transparent, fair, and merit-based - no candidate is required to pay money to any person for recruitment other than the nominal application fee.
Candidates are strongly advised to beware of touts and unscrupulous elements, and any such approach should be reported to the Recruitment Board or local police.

Selected candidates will be required to serve in the Force for a minimum period of 10 years; those who resign before this period must refund three months' pay/allowances or the cost of training, whichever is higher.

Persons belonging to physically handicapped categories are not eligible to apply for this post. Change of category will not be entertained at any later stage and will lead to cancellation of candidature. No Travelling Allowance (TA) is admissible for travel to the recruitment venue for any stage except the interview. All disputes will fall under the jurisdiction of the Hon'ble Courts within the territorial limits of Delhi only. For queries or clarifications, candidates may contact via email at [email protected]

Apply online only at https://recruitment.itbpolice.nic.in Applications received after the last date will not be accepted under any circumstances. Incomplete applications will be summarily rejected without any correspondence. Canvassing in any form will lead to cancellation of candidature. Change of category will not be entertained after submission of application. Mobile phones and electronic gadgets are strictly prohibited in the examination hall. Candidates must be present for more than one day at the recruitment centre under their own arrangements. All further notices/corrigendum will be published only on the official ITBP recruitment website.
